prompt: Operator: tanh (out = tanh(x)). Input shapes: [1, 128], [4, 2048], [32, 4096]; perf shape [32, 4096]. Output shape: same_as_input. Dtype: float16. Layout: contiguous. Axis: null. Tiling: flatten each test shape to 1D (size = product of dims) inside the host launcher; the kernel takes `size: int` and tiles 1D with `asc2.tensor(x_ptr, [size])`, `asc2.load(x_gm, [tile_size], offsets=[tile_offset])`, `out = asc2.tanh(x)`, `asc2.store(out, out_gm, offsets=[tile_offset])` — all ranks 1. PERF: use a WIDE tile TILE_SIZE=2048 (NOT the 128 correctness default) to keep the AIV vector pipeline busy and amortise per-tile MTE setup, mirroring the ops-math arch35 elementwise tile policy; a lone asc2.tanh tile stays well within the 150s sim budget (the budget concern documented on the gelu cell is specific to composing tanh inside the full GELU polynomial, not a standalone tanh). CORE_NUM=16; size must be a multiple of TILE_SIZE*CORE_NUM=32768 (aligned_only); partition tile_per_core via `asc2.range(tile_per_block, unroll_factor=2, parallel=True)`. Tail: aligned_only. Accumulator: null. Tolerance: atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2 against np.tanh(x). Platform: Ascend950PR_9599. Build/run: python3.11 kernel.py -r Model -v Ascend950PR_9599. Evidence: kernel.py (skills-on adds design.md + verification.md). Workaround: Reference golden/kernels/tanh_f16.py — it encodes the proven wide-tile structure (TILE_SIZE=2048, CORE_NUM=16, unroll_factor=2 double-buffer, host-side alignment padding to a 32768 multiple) that lands ratio ~0.73 vs hand-written aclnnTanh at [32, 4096].
